rulings.Case Title: GLADYS DEWITT VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 11, 2007, LINE 16, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY AND LADRON DURIO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEMURRER OF THE CITY AND DURIO IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND UNLESS PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE FACTS THAT DEMONSTRATE THAT THE TRAINING REFERRED TO IN PARAGRAPH 38 OF THE COMPLAINT WAS COMPLETED AFTER SEPTEMBER 20, 2005. ABSENT THAT, THE COMPLAINT IS BARRED ON ITS FACE BY GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 911.4. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BASED ON KEMMERER V. COUNTY OF FRESNO (1988) 200 CAL.APP.3D 1426, 1435. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO THE CITY IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BASED ON GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 818.8. THE THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO DURIO ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES AT THE HEARING THAT THERE IS A BASIS FOR DOING SO. =(302/PJM/LT-X)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA WONG VS. SUZANNE HOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 11, 2007, LINE 9, PLAINTIFF GLORIA WONG'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. ALTHOUGH THE OPPOSITION IS LATE, THE COURT CONSIDERED IT AND DENIES THE MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BECAUSE IT IS FAR FROM CLEAR THAT PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS GIVEN THE SUBMISSION OF THE TWO DIFFERENT CONTRACTS FOR THE PURCHASE OF THE BUSINESS. AS 10 PERCENT OWNERS OF THE PROPERTY, DEFENDANTS APPEAR TO HAVE A RIGHT TO BE ON THE PROPERTY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JULIO MESTRE VS. NATHANIEL P FORD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order Granting Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus Or Mandate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 11, 2007, LINE 10, PETITIONER JULIO MESTRE'S Motion For Order Granting Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus Or Mandate. THE WRIT IS DENIED. THE STEP 4 ARBITRATION IS NOT, BY ITS TERMS, A DE NOVO HEARING. THE EVIDENTIARY RECORD THAT MAKES UP THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD IS MORE THAN SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT PETITIONER, WHILE ON DUTY, MOLESTED A WOMAN AND AS A CONSEQUENCE OF HIS ARREST, PETITIONER FAILED TO COMPLETE HIS ASSIGNMENT ON JULY 2. ASSUMING ARGUENDO THAT THE ARBITRATOR IS CORRECT IN FINDING THAT MTA FAILED TO HOLD A TIMELY STEP 4 HEARING, THE DIRECTOR OF MTA HAS THE AUTHORITY UNDER THE MOU TO EXERCISE HIS DISRETION TO REJECT THE "ADVISORY STEP 4 OPINION AND AWARD." UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES, THE DIRECTOR OF MTA PROPERLY EXERCISED HIS DISCRETION. =(302/PJM/LT-X)

rulings.Case Title: CITI PROPERTIES DE, LLC VS. RANDY SOBENES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment And For Leave To Defend Action
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 11, 2007, LINE 12, DEFENDANTS RANDY SOBENES AND BRYAN QUALLS Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment And For Leave To Defend Action. DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS TIMELY. IT APPEARS THAT JUAN AND SUZANNE SOBENES LACK STANDING UNDER CCP SECTION 473.5 AS NEITHER WAS A PARTY. THE DEFENDANTS' DECLARATIONS FAIL TO ESTABLISH THAT A PARTY'S LACK OF NOTICE WAS NOT CAUSED BY AVOIDANCE OF SERVICE OR INEXUSABLE NEGLECT. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: IRMA SANCHEZ VS. MANUEL MUNOZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 11, 2007, LINE 18, DEFENDANTS MANUEL MUNOZ, MAIRA MUNOZ, AND LETICIA LANDAVERDE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CCP SECTION 827 NOTICE DID NOT BECOME EFFECTIVE UNTIL MARCH 15, 2007. AS A CONSEQUENCE, ALL THREE DEFENDANTS HAD THE RIGHT TO TENDER RENT IN JANUARY, FEBRUARY AND MARCH AND AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE NOTICE TO QUIT IS DEFECTIVE AND CANNOT BE CURED BY AMENDMENT TO THE COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM/PB)
